behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real Git rename detection plus CLI parsing/output. A unit test of category logic can pass while real `git` output (rename thresholds, status codes like `R100`, path formatting) is interpreted incorrectly. Integration is rejected because behavior text is about actual repository changes, not just internal mapping logic.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temp Git repo, commit a file, rename it, run the CLI, and assert report shows exactly one rename and zero add/delete entries for that file.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: Binary diff behavior depends on real Git/subprocess output for binary content (often special markers instead of text hunks). Mocks can hide decoding/subprocess edge failures, so real infra is required.
behaviors.test_description: In a real temp Git repo, add/modify a binary file (e.g., random bytes), run CLI against those changes, assert process exits successfully and binary file contributes to reported counts.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: Unborn-HEAD repositories trigger real Git error modes and exit codes. This failure appears at subprocess/repo-state boundary, not a single function boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a new real Git repo with no commits, run CLI, assert zero exit failure and an empty structured report shape.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: This is an end-to-end correctness claim against real repository history. Any fake history adapter can mask mismatches caused by actual Git command behavior, revision ranges, or parsing differences.
behaviors.test_description: Build a real repo history with known adds/deletes/renames/modifications across commits, run CLI for target range, and assert category counts equal the known ground truth.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: Requires real index vs working-tree semantics from Git. In-process fakes usually model only one state path and can miss staging-specific command/path differences.
behaviors.test_description: In a real temp repo, create equivalent change sets once staged and once unstaged, run CLI for each mode, and assert identical structured output.
